Bottom trawl survey of the Scotian Shelf, Bay of Fundy and Canadian portion of Georges Bank

Executive Summary:

The primary purpose of this survey is to gather data to evaluate the abundance and distribution of commercially exploited demersal or groundfish species, as well as non-commercial species, on Georges Bank, Bay of Fundy and Scotian Shelf. The data from this survey constitutes an important source of information on which to base recommendations for fishing quotas. For many of the marine species in this area, this survey provides the only source of information on their abundance and distribution, and how these have changed over time. This survey usually provides a platform for the collection of oceanographic data used by the Atlantic Zonal Monitoring Program (AZMP), but oceanographic sampling will be conducted aboard CCGS Capt Jacques Cartier during 2023 for the comparative fishing experiment. This multispecies bottom trawl survey is Core Mandate, it is an essential and required part of the strategy for the sustainable management of Maritimes Region's aquatic resources.
St. Andrews Biological Station - 2023 Maritimes Region Ecosystem Survey - Summer
